brief.headline_zh: EUIPO执法趋严与权利边界重塑
brief.headline_en: EUIPO Tightens Enforcement and Redefines Trademark Rights Boundaries
brief.summary_zh: 2026年上半年，EUIPO强化跨机构执法协作，重点打击药品及网络假冒。司法层面明确脱欧后英国未注册商标失效，并收紧名人姓名注册标准。同时，手工艺地理标志新制度落地，DesignView升级全球检索。中国企业需警惕执法风险，优化欧盟商标布局，并利用数字工具加强外观设计与品牌合规管理。
key_points: 执法协同升级：EUIPO联合欧盟委员会、Eurojust开展SHIELD VI等行动，严打药品及在线假冒，推广IPEP数字执法工具。
确权规则收紧：法院裁定脱欧过渡期后纯英国在先权利无效；名人姓名若被视为内容描述而非来源标识，将被拒绝注册。
制度工具革新：手工艺/工业产品地理标志（CIGI）进入过渡实施期；DesignView成为全球最大外观图像搜索引擎，提升检索透明度。
actions: 排查英国权利风险：复核依赖英国未注册商标阻碍欧盟注册的策略，确保在欧盟境内有独立有效的在先权利基础。
优化品牌注册策略：避免直接注册名人姓名或描述性词汇；利用SME基金补贴降低欧盟商标申请成本，实现27国覆盖。
加强外观与防伪合规：利用DesignView进行侵权比对自查；建立供应链溯源体系，应对欧盟针对药品及高科技产品的严厉海关查验。
watchlist: CIGI过渡期截止动态：关注2026年12月2日前成员国向欧盟新地理标志制度的转换进度及具体操作指南。
在线侵权治理新规：追踪EUIPO与欧盟委员会联合打击网络盗版后的配套平台责任界定及数据共享机制变化。
判例法适用口径：观察普通法院关于“Obelix”案后，对驰名商标跨类保护及声誉评估证据标准的后续司法实践倾向。
brief.summary_en: In the first half of 2026, the EUIPO intensified cross-agency enforcement collaboration, targeting pharmaceutical counterfeits and online infringement through initiatives like Operation SHIELD VI. Judicial developments clarified that UK unregistered rights cannot support EU trademark oppositions post-Brexit transition, while courts imposed stricter standards for registering celebrity names. Simultaneously, the new geographical indication system for craft and industrial products entered its transitional implementation phase, and DesignView expanded into the world’s largest global design image search engine. Rights holders must reassess reliance on UK-based prior rights, optimize EU trademark portfolios to avoid descriptive or celebrity-related refusals, and leverage enhanced digital tools for design clearance and supply chain compliance amidst heightened customs scrutiny.
key_points_en: Enforcement coordination escalated via Operation SHIELD VI and IPEP digital tools, focusing on seizing counterfeit medicines and disrupting online illicit sales networks.
Legal certainty tightened as courts ruled UK-only rights ineffective for EU oppositions post-transition and rejected celebrity names lacking distinct source-identifying function.
Systemic tools evolved with the Craft and Industrial Geographical Indications regime entering transition and DesignView becoming the premier global design image search platform.
actions_en: Audit portfolio strategies relying on UK unregistered rights to ensure independent, valid earlier rights exist within the EU for future opposition or invalidity actions.
Refine brand registration approaches by avoiding purely descriptive or celebrity-associated marks, while utilizing SME funds to secure cost-effective EU-wide trademark coverage.
Implement rigorous design clearance using DesignView’s expanded image search and establish robust supply chain traceability to mitigate risks from intensified customs enforcement.
watchlist_en: Monitor the December 2026 deadline for converting national craft geographical indications to the new EU unitary system and associated procedural guidelines.
Track evolving platform liability standards and data-sharing mechanisms resulting from joint EUIPO and European Commission actions against online piracy.
Observe judicial trends following the Obelix ruling regarding evidence standards for reputation assessment and cross-class protection of well-known trademarks.

items.title: Judgment of the General Court in Case T-24/25 Les Éditions Albert René v EUIPO - Works 11 Michał Lubiński (Obelix)
items.title_zh: 普通法院撤销EUIPO决定：武器类“Obelix”商标应无效
items.title_en: General Court Annuls EUIPO Refusal to Invalidate Obelix Mark for Weapons and Explosives
items.summary: The General Court nullifies EUIPO’s refusal to invalidate the word mark Obelix for weapons, ammunition and explosives
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟普通法院在T-24/25案中撤销EUIPO拒绝宣告“Obelix”武器商标无效的决定。出版商Les Éditions Albert René依据在先OBELIX商标及声誉受损申请无效，EUIPO曾以声誉证据不足为由驳回。法院指出EUIPO分析有误，未综合评估商标声誉及两商标间的联系。法院强调需全局考察在先商标显著性及公众联想，认定存在损害在先商标声誉的风险，支持了权利人的主张。
items.ai_summary_en: The General Court annulled an EUIPO decision refusing to invalidate the word mark Obelix for firearms, ammunition, and explosives. The applicant, Les Éditions Albert René, relied on its earlier OBELIX mark and alleged damage to its reputation. The Court found EUIPO’s assessment of the earlier mark’s reputation incomplete, noting it improperly disregarded evidence of use with the registered symbol and in association with Asterix. Furthermore, EUIPO failed to adequately assess the link between the marks and the risk of detriment to the earlier mark’s distinctive character or repute, despite differences in goods.
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment reinforces that EU trademark offices must conduct a holistic assessment of reputation, considering cumulative evidence rather than isolated deficiencies. Rights holders asserting cross-class protection based on reputation should ensure their submissions clearly demonstrate how distinct elements contribute to overall market recognition. The case highlights the importance of properly contextualizing co-branded or associated signs when establishing independent distinctiveness and repute for specific terms within a larger franchise.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["法院认定EUIPO对在先商标声誉评估不完整，忽视关键使用证据", "强调需全局评估商标间联系，不能仅因商品差异大而否定关联", "撤销EUIPO决定，支持基于声誉受损宣告争议商标无效的诉求"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["EUIPO erred by failing to comprehensively assess all relevant factors demonstrating the reputation of the earlier OBELIX mark.", "Evidence of use alongside the registered symbol and in combination with Asterix was unjustifiably disregarded in the reputation analysis.", "The assessment of the link between marks and potential detriment to reputation cannot rely solely on wide differences between goods."]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Rights holders must present comprehensive, cumulative evidence of reputation to withstand scrutiny in invalidity proceedings involving well-known cultural icons.", "Practice note: When assessing reputation, authorities must consider all relevant factors collectively; isolated insufficiencies do not negate established market recognition.", "What to monitor: Future applications of this holistic reputation assessment standard in cases involving associated signs or franchise-related trademarks."]
core_points_en: EUIPO erred by failing to comprehensively assess all relevant factors demonstrating the reputation of the earlier OBELIX mark.
Evidence of use alongside the registered symbol and in combination with Asterix was unjustifiably disregarded in the reputation analysis.
The assessment of the link between marks and potential detriment to reputation cannot rely solely on wide differences between goods.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Rights holders must present comprehensive, cumulative evidence of reputation to withstand scrutiny in invalidity proceedings involving well-known cultural icons.
Practice note: When assessing reputation, authorities must consider all relevant factors collectively; isolated insufficiencies do not negate established market recognition.
What to monitor: Future applications of this holistic reputation assessment standard in cases involving associated signs or franchise-related trademarks.

items.title: EUIPO supports major Europe-wide action against counterfeit medicines
items.title_zh: EUIPO支持欧洲打击假冒药品重大行动
items.title_en: EUIPO Supports Europol-Led Operation SHIELD VI Against Counterfeit Medicines
items.summary: EUIPO supports major Europe-wide action against counterfeit medicines
items.ai_summary_zh: EUIPO支持由Europol主导的“SHIELD VI”行动，30个欧洲国家参与，查获价值3200万欧元的假冒药品及非法兴奋剂。行动导致近500人被捕，关闭66个销售网站。EUIPO提供专业知识分析在线侵权趋势，并与EMA等机构联合发起宣传活动，警示从未经核实来源购买药品的风险，保护消费者及合法企业权益。
items.ai_summary_en: The European Union Intellectual Property Office (EUIPO) supported Operation SHIELD VI, a Europe-wide enforcement action led by Europol between April and November 2025. Authorities from 30 countries seized EUR 32 million in counterfeit medicines and illicit doping substances, including Semaglutide-based products. The operation resulted in nearly 500 arrests, over 3,300 prosecutions, and the shutdown of 66 websites selling fake pharmaceuticals. EUIPO contributed expertise in analyzing online infringement trends. Concurrently, EUIPO, the European Medicines Agency, and Europol launched the "Stay on top – EU vs Fake Medicines" campaign to educate consumers on identifying authorized sellers and avoiding unverified online sources.
items.ai_insight_en: This operation highlights the increasing convergence of IP enforcement and public health protection within the EU. For rights holders in the pharmaceutical sector, it underscores the critical role of data sharing with agencies like EUIPO and Europol. The focus on online platforms and specific high-demand substances, such as Semaglutide, indicates that brand owners should prioritize monitoring digital marketplaces for lookalike products. Collaborative initiatives like the "Stay on top" campaign offer strategic opportunities for companies to align with regulatory bodies, enhancing consumer trust while mitigating reputational risks associated with counterfeit goods.

items.ai_core_points_en: ["Operation SHIELD VI seized EUR 32 million in fake medicines across thirty European countries with EUIPO support.", "Enforcement actions led to nearly five hundred arrests and the closure of sixty-six illicit online sales websites.", "EUIPO and EMA launched a joint consumer awareness campaign to combat risks from unverified online medicine purchases."]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Pharmaceutical rights holders should leverage multi-agency collaborations to protect brand integrity and address public health risks posed by counterfeits.", "Practice note: EUIPO’s involvement demonstrates the value of providing infringement trend analysis to support coordinated cross-border enforcement actions.", "What to monitor: Rising online sales of counterfeit weight-loss and opioid products, and evolving guidance on authorized digital distribution channels."]

Enforcement actions led to nearly five hundred arrests and the closure of sixty-six illicit online sales websites.
EUIPO and EMA launched a joint consumer awareness campaign to combat risks from unverified online medicine purchases.

What to monitor: Rising online sales of counterfeit weight-loss and opioid products, and evolving guidance on authorized digital distribution channels.

items.title: Judgment of the Court of Justice in Case C-337/22 P EUIPO v Nowhere
items.title_zh: 欧盟法院：过渡期后仅依英国法享有的在先权利不得用于支持欧盟商标异议
items.title_en: CJEU Rules UK Rights Cannot Support EU Trademark Oppositions Post-Transition
items.summary: Brexit and EU trade marks: Earlier rights based solely on UK law can no longer be relied upon in support of an opposition where EUIPO rules after the end of the transition period
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟法院在EUIPO诉Nowhere案中撤销普通法院判决，维持EUIPO决定。法院裁定，若EUIPO在脱欧过渡期结束后作出裁决，仅依据英国法律享有的在先未注册商标权利，不再能作为反对欧盟商标注册的依据。因为在先权利必须在EUIPO作出决定时仍然有效，而英国权利在过渡期后在欧盟境内已失效。
items.ai_summary_en: The Court of Justice of the European Union set aside the General Court’s judgment in EUIPO v Nowhere, ruling that earlier rights based solely on UK law cannot support an opposition to an EU trademark application if the EUIPO decision is rendered after the Brexit transition period. Although the EU application was filed before the transition ended, the Court held that unregistered signs must confer a right to prohibit use at the time of the EUIPO decision. Since UK rights no longer have effect in the EU post-transition, they fail this second requirement. The Court thereby upheld the EUIPO’s rejection of the opposition, clarifying that the temporal validity of earlier rights is assessed at the date of the administrative decision, not merely the filing date.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案明确了脱欧后欧盟商标异议程序中“在先权利持续有效性”的时间判断标准。实务中需严格区分权利产生时间与权利存续时间，仅凭申请日前的英国使用事实不足以支撑过渡期后的欧盟异议，权利人需确保在EUIPO裁决时刻拥有欧盟境内有效的在先权利基础。
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment establishes that the substantive validity of earlier rights in EU trademark oppositions is determined at the time of the EUIPO decision. Rights holders cannot rely on national rights that have ceased to have effect in the EU territory due to Brexit, even if those rights existed when the contested application was filed. This reinforces the territorial nature of IP rights and the impact of geopolitical changes on pending proceedings.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧盟法院规定基于未注册标志的异议需满足两项要求：申请日前取得权利及EUIPO裁决时仍享…", "仅依英国法享有的在先权利在脱欧过渡期结束后，无法在欧盟境内继续产生禁止使用的法律效力", "即使欧盟商标申请提交于过渡期结束前，若EUIPO裁决时在先英国权利已失效，异议不能成立"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Earlier rights must confer prohibition rights at the time of the EUIPO decision, not just at filing.", "UK-only rights lost effect in the EU after the Brexit transition period expired.", "Oppositions relying solely on pre-transition UK unregistered rights fail if decided post-transition."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["明确EUIPO裁决时点在判断在先权利有效性中的决定性作用，而非仅看申请日", "纯英国法下的未注册商标权利在过渡期后彻底失去阻碍欧盟商标注册的程序资格", "后续需关注此类判决对涉及跨境未注册商标权益冲突案件的既判力影响"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Owners of UK-only unregistered rights cannot block later EU trademark registrations through oppositions decided after the transition period, regardless of prior…", "Practice note: In opposition proceedings, verify that cited earlier rights remain legally effective in the EU at the anticipated date of the EUIPO decision.", "What to monitor: Watch for similar applications of this temporal validity rule in other contexts where national rights diverge from EU protection scopes."]
core_points_en: Earlier rights must confer prohibition rights at the time of the EUIPO decision, not just at filing.
UK-only rights lost effect in the EU after the Brexit transition period expired.
Oppositions relying solely on pre-transition UK unregistered rights fail if decided post-transition.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Owners of UK-only unregistered rights cannot block later EU trademark registrations through oppositions decided after the transition period, regardless of prior…
Practice note: In opposition proceedings, verify that cited earlier rights remain legally effective in the EU at the anticipated date of the EUIPO decision.
What to monitor: Watch for similar applications of this temporal validity rule in other contexts where national rights diverge from EU protection scopes.

items.title: Lenços de Namorados do Minho the first Portuguese craft GI at EU level
items.title_zh: 米尼奥恋人巾成为欧盟首个葡萄牙手工艺地理标志
items.title_en: Lenços de Namorados do Minho Becomes First Portuguese Craft GI at EU Level
items.summary: The traditional embroidered handkerchief is the first Portuguese product name registered under the EU system for craft and industrial geographical indications and it is now protected across all EU Member States.
items.ai_summary_zh: 传统刺绣手帕“米尼奥恋人巾”（Lenços de Namorados do Minho）已作为首个葡萄牙产品名称，在欧盟手工艺和工业地理标志体系下完成注册。该注册意味着该产品名称现在在所有欧盟成员国境内均受到法律保护。
items.ai_summary_en: The European Union Intellectual Property Office has registered Lenços de Namorados do Minho, a traditional embroidered handkerchief, as the first Portuguese product under the new EU system for craft and industrial geographical indications. This registration grants the product name legal protection across all EU Member States. The milestone marks the initial implementation of the specialized GI framework for non-agricultural craft and industrial goods within the Union, extending uniform protection mechanisms previously associated primarily with agricultural products to this distinct category of traditional manufacturing.
items.ai_insight_en: This registration validates the operational status of the EU craft and industrial GI regime. For rights holders in traditional crafts, it confirms that cross-border protection is now accessible via this centralized mechanism. Stakeholders should observe how EUIPO applies examination standards to craft-specific criteria and how enforcement evolves in national jurisdictions, as this case sets an early precedent for the sector.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["米尼奥恋人巾是首个在欧盟手工艺和工业地理标志体系下注册的葡萄牙产品", "该地理标志注册后，产品名称在所有欧盟成员国境内获得统一保护"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Lenços de Namorados do Minho is the first Portuguese craft GI registered under the new EU system.", "The registration provides uniform legal protection for the product name across all EU Member States."]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Producers of traditional craft goods can now seek unitary EU-level protection, reducing the need for fragmented national registrations.", "Practice note: This case establishes an early benchmark for how EUIPO evaluates link-to-territory and quality criteria for non-agricultural craft products.", "What to monitor: Track subsequent filings from other Member States to gauge processing times and emerging examination trends for craft and industrial GIs."]
core_points_en: Lenços de Namorados do Minho is the first Portuguese craft GI registered under the new EU system.
The registration provides uniform legal protection for the product name across all EU Member States.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Producers of traditional craft goods can now seek unitary EU-level protection, reducing the need for fragmented national registrations.
Practice note: This case establishes an early benchmark for how EUIPO evaluates link-to-territory and quality criteria for non-agricultural craft products.
What to monitor: Track subsequent filings from other Member States to gauge processing times and emerging examination trends for craft and industrial GIs.

items.title: IPEP Presented in Athens: Strengthening IP Enforcement Through Digital Tools
items.title_zh: 雅典展示IPEP：以数字工具强化知识产权保护
items.title_en: EUIPO Presents IPEP Digital Tools to Strengthen IP Enforcement in Greece
items.summary: IPEP Presented in Athens: Strengthening IP Enforcement Through Digital Tools
items.ai_summary_zh: 2026年2月27日，欧盟知识产权局（EUIPO）在希腊雅典支持举办现场执法人员培训。活动由希腊市场控制局与全球反假冒集团共同组织，旨在通过IPEP及其移动应用提升一线官员识别假冒商品能力。约15家权利人企业分享汽配、时尚及烟草领域的打假实务，促进当局与权利人间的实时信息交换与合作。
items.ai_summary_en: The EUIPO presented its IP Enforcement Portal (IPEP) during a training session for frontline officers in Athens on 27 February 2026. Co-organized by Greek market control authorities and the Global Anti-Counterfeiting Group, the event targeted customs and police officials responsible for detecting counterfeit goods. Approximately fifteen rights holders shared practical guidance on identifying fakes in sectors such as automotive parts, fashion, and tobacco. The EUIPO showcased IPEP’s capabilities for secure, real-time information exchange and introduced the IPEP Mobile App, which enables multilingual rights searches and suspicious case notifications. This initiative aims to enhance cooperation between national authorities and industry stakeholders through digital enforcement tools.
items.ai_insight_en: This event highlights the EUIPO’s strategy to digitize enforcement cooperation at the national level. For rights holders, active participation in such training offers a channel to educate customs and police on specific product identifiers. The deployment of the IPEP Mobile App suggests a shift toward faster, field-based verification processes. Companies operating in Greece should monitor the adoption of these digital tools by local authorities, as effective use can streamline detention procedures and reduce the time required for initial infringement assessments.

items.title: Pro Bono Legal Support for SMEs: Call for Design Rights Experts as demand grows
items.title_zh: EUIPO呼吁外观设计专家加入中小企业公益法律援助项目
items.title_en: EUIPO Seeks Design Rights Experts for Pro Bono SME Support Programme
items.summary: Pro Bono Legal Support for SMEs: Call for Design Rights Experts as demand grows
items.ai_summary_zh: EUIPO公益项目2025年收到1375份中小企业支持请求，创历史新高，超九成来自微型企业。尽管商标咨询仍占主导，但2026年将重点加强外观设计保护领域的专家参与。目前网络已有289名活跃专家，主要来自德、意、西等国。EUIPO正招募具有外观设计权利经验的专业人士，以支持欧洲创意企业发展。
items.ai_summary_en: The EUIPO Pro Bono Programme recorded 1,375 SME support requests in 2025, the highest volume since its inception. Over 90% of applicants were microenterprises, with significant participation from Germany, Spain, Italy, France, and the Netherlands. While trademark assistance remains the primary demand, the programme is expanding its focus for 2026 to address growing needs in design protection. The network currently comprises 289 active experts, having added 165 new members in 2025. The EUIPO explicitly calls for IP practitioners with design rights expertise to join the network, aiming to strengthen support for design-driven businesses and improve user experience for both SMEs and professionals.
items.ai_insight_en: This expansion signals a strategic shift toward supporting design-intensive industries within the EU pro bono framework. For rights holders, it offers a viable channel for early-stage design strategy validation. Practitioners should note the opportunity to engage with emerging design-driven SMEs, potentially fostering long-term professional relationships through initial pro bono consultations.

items.title: EUIPO holds first Plenary Meeting of the Advisory Board for Craft and Industrial Geographical Indications
items.title_zh: EUIPO召开手工艺和工业地理标志咨询委员会首次全会
items.title_en: EUIPO Convenes First Plenary of Advisory Board for Craft and Industrial Geographical Indications
items.summary: EUIPO holds first Plenary Meeting of the Advisory Board for Craft and Industrial Geographical Indications
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟知识产权局（EUIPO）召开了手工艺和工业地理标志（CIGIs）咨询委员会的首次全体会议，标志着欧盟保护手工艺和工业地理标志新框架实施的重要里程碑。会议选举了主席和副主席，讨论了委员会的任务和工作方法，并初步讨论了相关指南，以确保新法规在欧盟范围内的高效、一致应用。
items.ai_summary_en: The European Union Intellectual Property Office held the inaugural plenary meeting of the Advisory Board for Craft and Industrial Geographical Indications. This event marks a significant milestone in implementing the new EU framework for protecting these specific indications. The Board elected its leadership and discussed its mandate, working methods, and national implementation progress. Members also initiated discussions on guidelines to ensure consistent application of the Regulation across the Union. The Board will now operate fully to support EUIPO’s new competences, providing expert opinions on quality criteria, reputation, and genericness while facilitating cooperation among Member States and the Commission.
items.ai_insight_en: The Advisory Board’s activation provides a critical expert layer for assessing craft and industrial GI applications. Rights holders should note the Board’s role in evaluating quality criteria and reputation, which may influence examination outcomes. Monitoring forthcoming guidelines and Board opinions will be essential for understanding evidentiary standards and ensuring consistent procedural expectations across Member States during the transition to the new regulatory framework.

items.title: Amplifying the fight against organised crime: EMPACT Communicators Network at EUIPO
items.title_zh: 强化打击有组织犯罪：EUIPO举办EMPACT通讯网络会议
items.title_en: EUIPO Hosts EMPACT Communicators Network to Amplify Fight Against Organised Crime
items.summary: Amplifying the fight against organised crime: EMPACT Communicators Network at EUIPO
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟知识产权局在阿利坎特主办欧盟打击刑事威胁多学科平台（EMPACT）通讯网络会议。来自欧盟各地的传播专家齐聚，旨在加强向公众解释打击严重和有组织犯罪的联合行动。会议聚焦于提升EMPACT作为欧盟执法合作基石的可见度，协调信息并分享最佳实践，以支持2026-2029新运营周期的政治势头和公众信心。
items.ai_summary_en: The European Union Intellectual Property Office hosted the EMPACT Communicators Network in Alicante, gathering communication experts to strengthen public messaging on joint actions against serious and organised crime. As EMPACT enters its 2026–2029 operational cycle, participants focused on sustaining political momentum, supporting operational excellence, and reinforcing public confidence. The network aims to align messages and share best practices to enhance the visibility of EU law enforcement cooperation. EMPACT serves as the EU’s flagship framework for coordinating investigations into threats such as trafficking, fraud, cybercrime, and intellectual property crime, ensuring operational achievements are understood beyond specialist audiences.
items.ai_insight_zh: 此次会议侧重于执法合作的战略传播与公众认知，而非具体的法律程序或判例更新。实务中应关注EMPACT框架下针对知识产权犯罪的跨境执法趋势，以及欧盟机构如何通过统一叙事强化对内部市场保护的公众支持。这反映了欧盟层面将IP执法纳入更广泛的安全与犯罪治理议程的政策导向。
items.ai_insight_en: This event underscores the integration of IP enforcement within broader EU security frameworks. For rights holders, it signals continued institutional priority on combating organised IP crime through coordinated multi-agency action. Monitoring EMPACT’s communication strategy offers insight into how EU agencies plan to publicize enforcement results and maintain political support for internal market protection measures during the new operational cycle.

items.title: Intellectual Property for all: The critical importance of IP rights to SMEs
items.title_zh: 知识产权惠及各方：权利对中小企业的关键重要性
items.title_en: EUIPO Commentary: Critical Role of IP Rights for SME Revenue and Investment
items.summary: Intellectual Property for all: The critical importance of IP rights to SMEs
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟知识产权局文章强调知识产权对欧盟中小企业至关重要。引用EUIPO与EPO联合研究显示，拥有知识产权的企业人均收入更高，中小企业注册知识产权后人均收入通常高出44%。知识产权密集型行业吸引了欧盟绝大多数风险投资，且初创企业若有注册商标或专利，获得早期风投的可能性高出十倍，成功退出的可能性高出200%。
items.ai_summary_en: The EUIPO highlights the strategic value of intellectual property for small and medium-sized enterprises, citing joint studies with the EPO. Data indicates that SMEs registering IP rights achieve 44% higher revenue per employee compared to non-IP holders. Furthermore, IP-intensive industries attracted 88% of private equity and venture capital funding in the EU between 2021 and 2023. These sectors also demonstrate a significant wage premium. The commentary underscores IP as a central asset for commercial success, competitiveness, and securing investment, aligning with broader European Commission reports on single market competitiveness and innovation signals for startups.
items.ai_insight_en: This commentary quantifies the commercial impact of IP registration for SMEs, linking protected assets to superior revenue metrics and access to venture capital. For in-house counsel and advisors, these statistics provide empirical support for prioritizing IP strategy in early-stage business planning. The data suggests that IP rights function not merely as legal protections but as critical financial signals to investors. Companies should view IP portfolio development as integral to valuation and growth strategies, particularly when seeking external funding in competitive EU markets.

items.title: New EU System for Geographical Indications for Craft and Industrial Products – important reminder for applicants
items.title_zh: EUIPO 提醒申请人关注欧盟手工艺品和工业产品地理标志新制度过渡安排
items.title_en: EUIPO Reminder on New EU Geographical Indications System for Craft and Industrial Products
items.summary: New EU System for Geographical Indications for Craft and Industrial Products – important reminder for applicants
items.ai_summary_zh: EUIPO 提醒申请人，自 2025 年 12 月 1 日起，该局已负责新的手工艺品和工业产品地理标志（CIGI）注册；到 2026 年 12 月 2 日，现有各成员国的相关国家保护制度将被新的欧盟制度取代。二十个成员国的申请人通常需先经本国主管机关完成国家阶段审查，其余七个成员国的申请人则直接向 EUIPO 申请。
items.ai_summary_en: Since 1 December 2025, the EUIPO has managed registrations for Geographical Indications for Craft and Industrial Products (CIGIs). National protection systems will be fully replaced by this EU framework by 2 December 2026. Applicants in twenty Member States must file through national authorities for initial examination and opposition, while applicants in seven specific states file directly with the EUIPO. The Office also accepts applications for existing national CIGIs protected or established by use before the competence transfer date. Early uptake is significant, with sixty-four applications published by late February 2026, primarily covering stones, textiles, and ceramics. The EUIPO provides the GIportal e-filing tool and educational resources via its GI Hub to support stakeholders during this transition.
items.ai_insight_zh: 这是一条实务价值很高的制度落地提醒。对涉及陶瓷、石材、纺织等传统产业的权利人和顾问而言，关键不是原则性介绍，而是尽快确认适用路径、时间窗口和是否需要把既有国家保护迁移到新欧盟体系内。
items.ai_insight_en: This transition creates immediate procedural obligations for rights holders in craft and industrial sectors. Companies must verify whether their jurisdiction requires national pre-screening or permits direct EUIPO filing to avoid procedural delays. Existing national rightsholders should prioritize converting protections to the EU register before the 2026 deadline to maintain continuous coverage. The early volume of applications in traditional sectors suggests active portfolio migration, making timely engagement with the GIportal system essential for effective rights management.

Filing routes differ: twenty Member States require national phase examination, while seven states allow direct application to the EUIPO.
Rights holders can seek EU protection for existing CIGIs established under national law or by use prior to 1 December 2025.

items.title: The EUIPO and the European Commission join forces to fight counterfeiting and piracy online

items.title_en: EUIPO and European Commission Announce Joint Action Against Online Counterfeiting
items.summary: The EUIPO and the European Commission join forces to fight counterfeiting and piracy online
items.ai_summary_zh: 原文仅包含标题及重复摘要，未提供正文细节。事实显示欧盟知识产权局（EUIPO）与欧盟委员会（EC）宣布建立合作关系，旨在共同应对在线环境中的假冒商品和盗版行为。
items.ai_summary_en: The European Union Intellectual Property Office (EUIPO) and the European Commission have announced a collaborative initiative to combat online counterfeiting and piracy. This joint effort signifies a coordinated approach by key EU institutions to address intellectual property infringements in the digital environment. The announcement confirms the alignment of resources and policy focus between the EUIPO and the Commission. However, the available source material is limited to this high-level declaration and does not provide specific details regarding operational mechanisms, targeted platforms, or implementation timelines. Rights holders should note this formal commitment to enhanced cooperation as a potential precursor to more detailed enforcement frameworks or guidance in the near future.
items.ai_insight_en: This announcement establishes a clear institutional priority for combating online IP infringement at the EU level. While specific measures are not yet detailed, the collaboration suggests a unified front that may influence future enforcement trends. Practitioners should track subsequent communications for concrete tools or reporting channels that could assist rights holders in monitoring and addressing digital violations more effectively across member states.

items.title: Fake toner, real trouble: protect your printers from counterfeits
items.title_zh: UKIPO警示：假冒碳粉危害大，需强化防伪保护
items.title_en: UKIPO Warns of Risks from Counterfeit Ink and Toner Cartridges
items.summary: Counterfeit ink and toner cartridges can be poor quality and can cause serious problems, especially for small businesses.
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国知识产权局（UKIPO）发布博客文章，警示假冒墨盒和碳粉对中小企业造成的严重损害。文章指出全球假冒贸易规模巨大且常关联有组织犯罪，电商平台第三方卖家易成售假渠道。UKIPO联合ICCE等机构开展执法与情报协调，强调造假者不承担赔偿责任。建议企业建立严格采购体系，通过比对正品特征识别假货，以规避法律与经济损失风险。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK Intellectual Property Office highlights the significant risks posed by counterfeit ink and toner cartridges, particularly for small businesses. Citing an OECD/EUIPO report estimating global counterfeiting trade at USD 467 billion, the agency notes strong links to organized crime. The blog emphasizes that online marketplaces facilitate the sale of fake goods by third-party sellers, often at lower prices. Unlike genuine manufacturers, counterfeiters do not accept liability for damage caused to printers or other equipment. The IPO collaborates with industry groups like the Imaging Consumables Coalition of Europe (ICCE) to coordinate enforcement and raise awareness about identifying fake products through quality checks.
items.ai_insight_zh: 在欧中国打印耗材出口企业应警惕被卷入假冒供应链指控。需建立严格的知识产权合规与溯源体系，主动配合品牌方打假。同时，进口商应加强供应商尽职调查，避免采购来源不明的低价耗材，以防因销售假冒产品面临UKIPO执法行动及民事索赔风险。
items.ai_insight_en: This update underscores the operational and financial exposure businesses face when procuring unverified printing supplies. For in-house counsel and procurement teams, the absence of liability coverage from counterfeiters represents a distinct risk profile compared to standard supply chain disputes. It reinforces the need for strict vendor due diligence and authentication protocols within corporate purchasing policies to mitigate hardware damage and potential association with illicit trade networks.

items.title_en: DesignView Becomes World’s Largest Design Image Search Engine Following Global Office Integration
items.summary: DesignView becomes the world’s largest design image search engine As of 9 February 2026, the DesignView search functionality for industrial design images has been implemented for all connected intellectual property (IP) offices, making it the largest search engine for design images in the world. Wit
items.ai_summary_zh: 截至2026年2月9日，随着所有连接的知识产权局实施工业设计图像搜索功能，DesignView已成为全球最大的外观设计图像搜索引擎。该更新由欧盟知识产权局（EUIPO）发布，标志着其全球数据覆盖范围的进一步扩大。
items.ai_summary_en: As of 9 February 2026, the European Union Intellectual Property Office (EUIPO) announced that DesignView has become the world’s largest search engine for industrial design images. This milestone follows the implementation of design image search functionality across all connected intellectual property offices. The update significantly expands the platform's data coverage, enabling users to access a comprehensive global repository of design representations. By integrating image search capabilities from all participating jurisdictions, DesignView now offers enhanced visibility into prior art and registered designs worldwide, supporting more robust clearance and novelty assessments for rights holders and practitioners operating in international markets.
items.ai_insight_en: This expansion enhances the utility of DesignView for prior art searches and novelty assessments by consolidating global design imagery into a single interface. Practitioners should leverage this broader dataset to improve the thoroughness of clearance searches. While the source does not detail algorithmic specifics, the increased volume of accessible images necessitates careful review of search results to ensure relevant prior designs are identified during due diligence processes.

items.title: European Union Trademark: A Malta Perspective
items.title_zh: 欧盟商标：马耳他视角下的保护策略
items.title_en: European Union Trademark Registration: Strategic Options from a Malta Perspective
items.summary: Malta's dual trademark protection system offers businesses strategic flexibility through national registration or the European Union Trademark route. The EUTM framework, governed by Regulation (EU) 2017/1001, provides cost-effective protection across 27 EU countries through a single application, while Malta's position as an English-speaking jurisdiction enhances its role in IP portfolio management and cross-border structuring.
items.ai_summary_zh: 马耳他提供国家注册与欧盟商标（EUTM）双轨保护。EUTM依据第2017/1001号条例，通过单一申请覆盖27个欧盟成员国，具有成本效益。马耳他作为英语司法管辖区，有利于知识产权组合管理。文章回顾了从共同体商标到EUTM的法律演变，并分析了单一注册、统一续展及语言优势等实务要点。
items.ai_summary_en: Malta offers dual trademark protection pathways through national registration or the European Union Trademark (EUTM) system. Governed by Regulation (EU) 2017/1001, the EUTM framework replaces the former Community Trademark regime, providing cost-effective coverage across twenty-seven EU member states via a single application. This analysis highlights Malta’s role as an English-speaking jurisdiction within the EU, facilitating streamlined IP portfolio management and cross-border structuring for international businesses. The EUTM mechanism simplifies administrative burdens by consolidating registration forms, renewal dates, and fee structures, while allowing applications in English with a second language designation for procedural communications.
items.ai_insight_en: This analysis underscores the operational efficiency of the EUTM system for rights holders managing pan-European brands. By leveraging Malta’s English-language legal environment, companies can streamline portfolio administration. The shift from national to unitary rights reduces fragmentation, though applicants must balance broad coverage against the risk of central refusal affecting all designated territories.

items.ai_summary_en: The European Union Intellectual Property Office (EUIPO) and the European Investment Fund (EIF) have announced a collaborative initiative aimed at unlocking finance backed by intellectual property rights. This partnership seeks to address financing challenges for innovative companies by leveraging intangible assets, such as trademarks and patents, as collateral or value indicators. The cooperation reflects a broader EU effort to improve access to capital for knowledge-intensive enterprises through structured financial mechanisms involving public institutions.
items.ai_insight_en: For portfolio managers and corporate counsel, this development highlights the growing institutional recognition of IP as a tangible financial asset. Rights holders should note that official bodies are actively working to standardize how IP contributes to creditworthiness assessments. While specific operational guidelines are not yet detailed, the involvement of the EIF suggests potential future instruments or guarantees that could impact how SMEs structure their balance sheets and seek external funding based on their trademark and patent portfolios.

items.ai_summary_en: The German Patent and Trade Mark Office (DPMA) marks World Intellectual Property Day with a campaign against product piracy, focusing on the theme of intellectual property and sport. DPMA President Eva Schewior urges consumers to exercise caution when purchasing sports merchandise, warning that counterfeit goods support organized crime and pose health risks. To aid identification, the DPMA has published new informational pages detailing signs of fake products, such as unusual textile gloss or unrealistic pricing. The initiative also addresses the role of influencers promoting counterfeit items. These resources aim to clarify the economic and social damages caused by piracy, including job losses and reduced competitiveness, while highlighting enforcement efforts across Germany.
items.ai_insight_en: This initiative reflects a broader European regulatory trend linking IP enforcement to public interest concerns like consumer safety and fair competition. Rights holders should note the DPMA’s emphasis on digital channels, particularly influencer marketing, as a key vector for counterfeits. The provided identification guides offer practical benchmarks for brand protection teams to enhance consumer education materials. Monitoring how national offices leverage international data from bodies like the OECD and EUIPO can help anticipate shifts in enforcement priorities and public messaging strategies regarding IP infringement.
